{"key_event":"Civic vetoes amid presidentialized security state","reasoning":"Civil society remained very strong, as the 2005 EU referendum and 2006 CPE mobilization showed real veto power, but declining unions, suburban fragmentation, and expanded security powers slightly reduced cohesion and liberties. State power edged up through presidentialization, policing/anti-terror capacity, and immigration-control agenda-setting, despite EU constraints, decentralization, and modest 2008 parliamentary/rights checks.","society_change":-0.1,"society_power":8.8,"state_change":0.1,"state_power":8.6}
